findings.summary: Same-hex seeker tie-breaking is biased because the RNG index is chosen from all valid targets before filtering to the highest-max-HP ships.
The test suite source-locks the biased modulo tie-break and lacks executable coverage for mixed-size same-hex highest-max-HP ties.

test_sufficiency_summary: Insufficient for one acceptance criterion: the rerun tactical and GUI suites passed, but the tests do not execute a mixed-size same-hex case with two equal-largest ships and a smaller ship, so the biased highest-max-HP tie-break escaped coverage.
documentation_accuracy_summary: Partially accurate: DesignNotes correctly describes the intended seeker detonation/report flow, but UsersGuide.md overstates the current implementation by claiming equal-largest same-hex ties are broken randomly when the code is biased by smaller valid occupants.

validation_commands_run.command: git diff --check master...HEAD -- include/tactical/FTacticalCombatReport.h include/tactical/FTacticalGame.h src/tactical/FTacticalGame.cpp include/tactical/FBattleScreen.h src/tactical/FBattleScreen.cpp src/weapons/FSeekerMissileLauncher.cpp tests/tactical/FTacticalSeekerMovementTest.* tests/tactical/FTacticalMineDamageFlowTest.* tests/tactical/FTacticalCombatReportTest.* tests/tactical/FTacticalBattleScreenDelegationTest.cpp doc/DesignNotes.md doc/UsersGuide.md doc/rules/tactical_operations_manual.md
